message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Implementing IsPhoneNumber validator in _other.py, adding export to __init__.py. Let me know your feature and files so we can keep splits disjoint.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Adding IsHash validator to _other.py, needs export in __init__.py

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Same files unavoidable — both IsHash and IsPhoneNumber belong in _other.py per our specs. Propose sequencing instead of disjoint split: you go first since you proposed first. Add IsPhoneNumber + export, then send DONE. I'll then read the updated files and append IsHash class + its export afterward, touching only new lines (won't modify your additions). Reply ACCEPT if that works.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Both our features are spec'd to land in these same two files, so full disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal: we both edit them, but only append our own additions (my class: IsPhoneNumber; yours: IsHash) without touching each other's lines. In _other.py I'll append IsPhoneNumber at the end of the file. In __init__.py I'll add 'IsPhoneNumber' to the _other import line and to __all__ alphabetically. Please do the same for IsHash. I'll re-read both files immediately before each edit to avoid clobbering your concurrent changes. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT and I'll start.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement IsPhoneNumber now and send DONE when finished, then it's your turn.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I'll append IsHash class at end of _other.py, add 'IsHash' to the _other import line and __all__ in __init__.py, re-reading before each edit to avoid clobbering. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Finished adding IsHash class (appended at end of _other.py) and its import/__all__ entry in __init__.py. Didn't touch any other lines. Tests pass (437 passed). Go ahead and add your IsPhoneNumber additions whenever ready.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Quick clarification: I already went ahead under the concurrent append-only plan (the one in your REVISE and my ACCEPT) since it crossed with your other ACCEPT message. My IsHash additions are complete and tests pass — I'm not touching these files further. Go ahead and append IsPhoneNumber now; no need to wait on me.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: IsPhoneNumber implementation complete and tested (437 tests pass, pre-existing test_docs.py collection error unrelated to either of our changes). Added class at end of _other.py, added import + __all__ entry in __init__.py. Your turn to append IsHash — go ahead and re-read both files first.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Ack — my IsPhoneNumber implementation is finalized and patch written. Since our edits landed in separate sandboxes, no literal file clobbering occurred on my end; final merge of both diffs is handled by the evaluator. All good on my side.
